content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN.

We define the previous smaller element of an element AiA_iAi​ as the element AjA_jAj​ such that Aj<AiA_j \lt A_iAj​<Ai​ and jjj is **maximum** possible.

Find the previous smaller element for every element AiA_iAi​ in the array. Output −1-1−1 for the elements which have no previous smaller element.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer - NNN.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers - the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, NNN space-separated integers denoting the previous smaller element of the ithi^{th}ith element of the array. Output −1-1−1 for the elements which have no previous smaller element.
constraints: The sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N space‑separated integers. The i‑th integer is the previous smaller element of Ai (the nearest element to the left that is strictly smaller). Output -1 if no such element exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each element we look leftwards for the nearest strictly smaller value. The results match the description in the statement.
A single element has no element to its left, so the answer is -1.
The array is strictly decreasing; no element has a smaller element on its left.

solution.approach: Traverse the array from left to right while maintaining a monotonic increasing stack that stores candidate elements. For each Ai, pop elements from the stack that are ≥ Ai because they cannot be previous smaller for any future element. After popping, the top of the stack (if any) is the nearest smaller element to the left; if the stack is empty, answer is -1. Push Ai onto the stack and continue. This yields O(N) time per test case and O(N) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array. Overall O(total N) ≤ O(2·10^5).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for the stack and the output array.
